title: Asukas Last Stand
wikitext:
[]: Asuka's Last Stand             Quote Post by LonelyAsuka » Tue Feb 04, 2025 9:48 pm [spoiler=][/spoiler] Is anyone else nearly unable to watch Asuka's final moments in the end of Evangelion during her final battle? Everytime I see the scene or even her unit's corpse my heart sinks, when I rewatch it I always cry and contemplate just skipping the scene altogether.

[]: Re: Asuka's Last Stand             Quote Post by FelipeFritschF » Tue Feb 04, 2025 10:16 pm More or less, because I know that ultimately she does get a way out in the end. It's more the fact that her fight was somewhat futile on its own since she isn't able to defeat the MP Evas. Probably if Shinji did help her they could have accomplished that, but that would have created problems of its own. There would be no way for Misato to come back, for instance. What I do have a hard time rewatching is Asuka's memories in Episode 22.

[]: Re: Asuka's Last Stand             Quote Post by LonelyAsuka » Wed Feb 05, 2025 1:52 am FelipeFritschF wrote: More or less, because I know that ultimately she does get a way out in the end. It's more the fact that her fight was somewhat futile on its own since she isn't able to defeat the MP Evas. Probably if Shinji did help her they could have accomplished that, but that would have created problems of its own. There would be no way for Misato to come back, for instance. What I do have a hard time rewatching is Asuka's memories in Episode 22.

[]: Re: Asuka's Last Stand             Quote Post by Vqs88_C3H6O » Thu Feb 06, 2025 5:54 am LonelyAsuka wrote: FelipeFritschF#944474 wrote: More or less, because I know that ultimately she does get a way out in the end. It's more the fact that her fight was somewhat futile on its own since she isn't able to defeat the MP Evas. Probably if Shinji did help her they could have accomplished that, but that would have created problems of its own. There would be no way for Misato to come back, for instance. What I do have a hard time rewatching is Asuka's memories in Episode 22.

[]: Re: Asuka's Last Stand             Quote Post by LonelyAsuka » Thu Feb 06, 2025 7:46 am quote="Vqs88_C3H6O#944490"] LonelyAsuka#944475 wrote: That's another one I have trouble watching, it's hard to watch Asuka suffer in general because I relate to a lot of her problems, that being said it also doesn't help that we both have german heritage, the same hair color and same eye color.

[]: Re: Asuka's Last Stand             Quote Post by AtomicRambler » Mon Feb 10, 2025 10:20 pm Agreed. To me, that scene was unnecessarily sadistic in its brutality. Probably the single most disturbing thing I've seen (dating myself here, but I was less disturbed by my first viewing of Saving Private Ryan .) I get that the whole movie was supposed to be disturbing. But for me, EoE spent so much time staring into the abyss (of violence, cruelty, darkness, horror, despair) that it undermined whatever hopeful message it was trying to convey. I know part of it is that I'm a dad. My oldest is nearly 14. So seeing seeing these young teenage characters go through such absolute hell was incredibly difficult. I also had a sister who was, in some significant ways, similar to Asuka. So she as a character hit very close to home, and her brutal death — coming directly on the heels of her finally discovering that her mother had been with her the whole time — hit me very cruelly.

[]: Re: Asuka's Last Stand             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Tue Feb 11, 2025 2:45 pm It's a hard watch but I think it's fine. EoE is also where the characters feel the least like actual kids imo (they rarely do, but in EoE dialogue and such is just really removed from that). For me it's the opposite, if it didn't depict such dark stuff, the hopeful message wouldn't quite hit for me. Like "of course you're trying to sell me on rainbows, you don't know how bad it gets".

[]: Re: Asuka's Last Stand             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Feb 16, 2025 4:47 pm I can see how it would be challenging if you tend to be sensitive to dark stuff. That said people just have different sensitivities and that's ok. Also, apparently IIRC Asuka's Va also can't watch it. Which is kinda funny because one could easily picture Asuka herself as being the kind who'd be into shooter games & death metal.
